sections.paragraph:
μέντοι particle (Trag., Hdt.+; ins, pap, LXX; TestSol 11:9 C; TestJob; Jos., Ant. 14, 162, C. Ap. 1, 8; 13 al.—Denniston 397–412).

① really, actually Js 2:8.

② mostly adversative (cp. οὐ μέντοι γε ‘though not’ Just., D. 5, 1) though, to be sure, indeed οὐδεὶς μ. εἶπεν though no one said J 4:27; cp. 7:13. οὐ μ. though not, indeed not 20:5; 21:4; Hs 6, 1, 6 v.l.; Papias (2:15). ὁ μ. θεμέλιος ἕστηκεν nevertheless the firm foundation stands 2 Ti 2:19. ὅμως μ. yet, despite that (Kühner-G. II 280) J 12:42.—Weakened to but Jd 8 (B-D-F §450, 1; Rob. 1154; 1188).—M-M.

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Denniston Denniston = JD., The Greek Particles—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Papias Papias , early Christian writer, II a.d.—List 1

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 630
